sections.paragraph:
- Original: Κορνήλιος

- Transliteration: Kornelios

- Phonetic: kor-nay'-lee-os

- Definition: Cornelius = "of a horn"

1. a Roman centurion of the Italian cohort stationed in Caesarea who converted to Christianity

- Origin: of Latin origin

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: Of Latin original; Cornelius a Roman: - Cornelius.
